name: Makua Beach
tagline: The last beach before the road ends — remote, wild, and stunningly beautiful with spinner dolphins offshore.
parking: Roadside parking along Farrington Hwy; limited spaces
hours: Open daily, sunrise to sunset; road beyond Makua is gated
bestTime: Summer mornings for calm water and dolphin sightings; avoid after heavy rain
talkStory: This is about as remote as it gets on Oahu without hiking. The beach sits in a dramatic valley with steep cliffs and spinner dolphins are regularly spotted in the bay. Swimming is only safe in calm summer conditions — winter currents are dangerous. The area has deep cultural significance to Native Hawaiians, so treat it with extra respect. Don't leave any valuables in your car.
